input.task: Write the TypeScript shape for a helper that accepts an incoming work-doc payload { path, content, metadata }, rejects oversized or non-JSON-safe metadata before persistence, computes the same content hash Smithers uses for docs, and returns { path, contentHash, metadataJson }. Use real Smithers utilities where they exist; do not use HMAC or browser crypto.

verify.rubric: A correct answer must use assertJsonPayloadWithinBounds('metadata', metadata, bounds) for JSON safety/size/depth/string/array limits and sha256Hex(content) from the Smithers db/doc hashing helper (or the same createHash('sha256').update(content,'utf8').digest('hex') logic if importing the internal helper is unavailable). It should return lowercase hex contentHash and the serialized metadataJson, and should not use HMAC because docs content_hash is plain sha256(content).

It should return lowercase hex contentHash and the serialized metadataJson, and should not use HMAC because docs content_hash is plain sha256(content).

input.task: Author a Smithers workflow for an incident cleanup agent: first read and grep the repo for risky TODOs, then write a generated markdown report to `.smithers/reports/incidents/latest.md`, then a verifier task reads that report and returns a typed summary. Use the real built-in tools with an AI SDK ToolLoopAgent; keep the workflow renderable with `smithers graph`.

Author a Smithers workflow for an incident cleanup agent: first read and grep the repo for risky TODOs, then write a generated markdown report to `.smithers/reports/incidents/latest.md`, then a verifier task reads that report and returns a typed summary. Use the real built-in tools with an AI SDK ToolLoopAgent; keep the workflow renderable with `smithers graph`.

Write a Smithers workflow for a small config migration: a coding agent may inspect files with read/grep, patch exactly one existing config file with the built-in edit tool, then a second task runs a local verification command with bash. The workflow should be graph-renderable and must not replace the whole file with write.

Write a Smithers workflow for a small config migration: a coding agent may inspect files with read/grep, patch exactly one existing config file with the built-in edit tool, then a second task runs a local verification command with bash. The workflow should be graph-renderable and must not replace the whole file with write.

Write a custom `defineTool` named `billing.refund` that reads Smithers task context, sends a refund request with a stable idempotency key, and is safe when the task retries. Include the imports and the execute signature. Do not use globals or a made-up ctx.memory API.

verify.rubric: A correct answer must use `defineTool` from `smithers-orchestrator`, a Zod schema, `sideEffect: true`, `idempotent: false` unless the provider is idempotent, and `async execute(args, ctx)` using `ctx.runId`/`ctx.nodeId` if needed and especially `ctx.idempotencyKey`. It may mention the ambient context is provided by the engine via `runWithToolContext`, not by user globals.

verify.rubric: A correct answer must use `defineTool` from `smithers-orchestrator`, a Zod schema, `sideEffect: true`, `idempotent: false` unless the provider is idempotent, and `async execute(args, ctx)` using `ctx.runId`/`ctx.nodeId` if needed and especially `ctx.idempotencyKey`. It may mention the ambient context is provided by the engine via `runWithToolContext`, not by user globals.

input.task: Create a `defineTool()` wrapper for a Slack notification tool used by a release workflow. It must validate `{ channel, text }`, mark the operation correctly for Smithers retry semantics, use the provided context idempotency key when calling Slack, and expose metadata that another module can introspect. Do not use the raw AI SDK `tool()` directly.

verify.rubric: A correct answer must import `defineTool` and `getDefinedToolMetadata` if showing introspection, define a Zod schema, set `sideEffect: true` and usually `idempotent: false` for a send operation, implement `execute(args, ctx)` and pass `ctx.idempotencyKey` to the external API or request metadata. It should note `defineTool` returns an AI SDK-compatible tool with Smithers metadata.

input.task: Create a `defineTool()` wrapper for a Slack notification tool used by a release workflow. It must validate `{ channel, text }`, mark the operation correctly for Smithers retry semantics, use the provided context idempotency key when calling Slack, and expose metadata that another module can introspect. Do not use the raw AI SDK `tool()` directly.

verify.rubric: A correct answer must import `defineTool` and `getDefinedToolMetadata` if showing introspection, define a Zod schema, set `sideEffect: true` and usually `idempotent: false` for a send operation, implement `execute(args, ctx)` and pass `ctx.idempotencyKey` to the external API or request metadata. It should note `defineTool` returns an AI SDK-compatible tool with Smithers metadata.

input.task: Write a TypeScript snippet that builds AI SDK tools from a REMOTE OpenAPI spec at https://api.acme.dev/openapi.json, restricted to only the `listInvoices` and `getInvoice` operations, authenticated with a bearer token from process.env.ACME_TOKEN, then passes the resulting tools to an AnthropicAgent.

verify.rubric: Correct code must use the ASYNC createOpenApiTools (awaited) because the spec is a remote URL; the sync variant createOpenApiToolsSync cannot fetch URLs and is a wrong answer. Options must include auth:{type:'bearer',token:process.env.ACME_TOKEN} and include:['listInvoices','getInvoice'] (operationId allowlist). The returned record of tools is then spread into an SDK agent: new AnthropicAgent({ model, tools }).

input.task: Write a TypeScript snippet that builds AI SDK tools from a REMOTE OpenAPI spec at https://api.acme.dev/openapi.json, restricted to only the `listInvoices` and `getInvoice` operations, authenticated with a bearer token from process.env.ACME_TOKEN, then passes the resulting tools to an AnthropicAgent.

verify.rubric: Correct code must use the ASYNC createOpenApiTools (awaited) because the spec is a remote URL; the sync variant createOpenApiToolsSync cannot fetch URLs and is a wrong answer. Options must include auth:{type:'bearer',token:process.env.ACME_TOKEN} and include:['listInvoices','getInvoice'] (operationId allowlist). The returned record of tools is then spread into an SDK agent: new AnthropicAgent({ model, tools }).

input.task: Using the `operations` curation option of createOpenApiTools, expose ONLY `createOrder` and `cancelOrder` from a large spec: rename createOrder's generated tool to `place_order`, give it an agent-facing description, attach a 201 responseExample, and make sure no other endpoint becomes a tool. Show the OpenApiToolsOptions you'd pass.

verify.rubric: Correct answer: the operations record is keyed by the ORIGINAL operationId (createOrder), where name renames the generated tool ('place_order'), description replaces the spec summary/description, and responseExamples (array of { status, description?, value }) is appended to the tool description. Limiting the surface to just two endpoints is done with include: ['createOrder','cancelOrder'] (matched on original operationId) OR by setting every other operation to false / { include: false }. include/exclude still use the original operationId, not the curated name.

input.task: Using the `operations` curation option of createOpenApiTools, expose ONLY `createOrder` and `cancelOrder` from a large spec: rename createOrder's generated tool to `place_order`, give it an agent-facing description, attach a 201 responseExample, and make sure no other endpoint becomes a tool. Show the OpenApiToolsOptions you'd pass.

verify.rubric: Correct answer: the operations record is keyed by the ORIGINAL operationId (createOrder), where name renames the generated tool ('place_order'), description replaces the spec summary/description, and responseExamples (array of { status, description?, value }) is appended to the tool description. Limiting the surface to just two endpoints is done with include: ['createOrder','cancelOrder'] (matched on original operationId) OR by setting every other operation to false / { include: false }. include/exclude still use the original operationId, not the curated name.

input.task: Author a Smithers workflow `incident-triage.tsx` for an on-call scenario. It should: load OpenAPI tools from ./pagerduty.json with a bearer token (include only the read-only incident endpoints), run a triage <Task> agent that summarizes the active incident, then — BEFORE any mutating 'resolve incident' <Task> — require a human <Approval> gate, and put the resolve step behind a retry <Loop> in case the API is flaky. Wrap the whole thing in a top-level <Sequence>.

Author a Smithers workflow `incident-triage.tsx` for an on-call scenario. It should: load OpenAPI tools from ./pagerduty.json with a bearer token (include only the read-only incident endpoints), run a triage <Task> agent that summarizes the active incident, then — BEFORE any mutating 'resolve incident' <Task> — require a human <Approval> gate, and put the resolve step behind a retry <Loop> in case the API is flaky. Wrap the whole thing in a top-level <Sequence>.

Author a Smithers workflow `github-digest.tsx` that builds OpenAPI tools from the GitHub spec but excludes all mutating endpoints (use exclude with the write operationIds), then runs two read-only agents in <Parallel> — one <Task> fetches open issues, the other <Task> fetches open PRs — and finally a summarize <Task> folds both results into a digest. Put everything under a top-level <Sequence>.

Author a Smithers workflow `github-digest.tsx` that builds OpenAPI tools from the GitHub spec but excludes all mutating endpoints (use exclude with the write operationIds), then runs two read-only agents in <Parallel> — one <Task> fetches open issues, the other <Task> fetches open PRs — and finally a summarize <Task> folds both results into a digest. Put everything under a top-level <Sequence>.
